div.note: 031.11 American Museum of Natural History (4th Asiatic)/92
div.head: Memorandum by the Minister in China (Johnson)Copy transmitted to the Department without covering despatch; received June 19, 1930.
div.p: In the course of a conversation today with the Minister for Foreign Affairs, the latter volunteered the information that everything had been settled in connection with the expedition of Dr. Roy Chapman Andrews. He said that he was writing a formal letter concerning this matter very shortly. He said that everything was now settled.

I told him I was very glad to hear this and I would telegraph this information to Dr. Andrews who was very anxious to hear about the matter.The expedition to Mongolia in the summer of 1930 was the last one, for permission was not granted again in 1931 and further attempts were abandoned in 1932.
